revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Homi-jinja (Kushigata)]][[ja:穂見神社 (南アルプス市)]] {{Expand Japanese|穂見神社 (南アルプス市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Homi Shrine | native_name = 穂見神社 (南アルプス市) | image = HomijinnjyaIMG 1221.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Homi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.62169444|138.40561111}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi|bcl|Ukemochi|ca|Uke Mochi|da|Ogetsu-no-hime|en|Ukemochi|eo|Uke Moĉi|es|Uke Mochi|eu|Ukemochi|fa|اوکه موچی|hr|Uke Mochi|it|Uke-Mochi|ja|保食神|mni|ꯎꯀꯦꯃꯣꯆꯤ|pl|Ukemochi|pt|Ukemochi|ru|Укэмоти-но ками|sl|Uke-moči|sv|Uke-Mochi|uk|Укемоті|zh|保食神|qid=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Homi Shrine'''|穂見神社 (南アルプス市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kai Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11577868|ltq=Q11577868|qid=Q11577868|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kai Province}} ** Series ordinal: 11 ** P155: {{ill|Kamuheno Shrine|lt=Kamuheno Shrine|qid=Q135039697|1=Kamuheno Shrine (Q135039697)}} ** P156: {{ill|Q111495856|ltq=Q111495856|qid=Q111495856|1=Uwato Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q824000|ltq=Q824000|qid=Q824000|1=Minami-Alps, Yamanashi}} * {{ill|Kai Province|lt=Kai Province|ckb|پارێزگای کای|cs|Provincie Kai|de|Provinz Kai|en|Kai Province|eo|Provinco Kai|es|Provincia de Kai|eu|Kai probintzia|fa|ولایت کای|fi|Kain provinssi|fr|Province de Kai|gl|Provincia de Kai|id|Provinsi Kai|it|Kai (provincia)|ja|甲斐国|ko|가이국|lt|Kajaus žemė|ms|Wilayah Kai|nl|Kai (provincie)|pt|Província de Kai|ru|Каи (провинция)|simple|Kai Province|sk|Kai (provincia)|sr|Провинција Каи|tr|Kai (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Кай|wuu|甲斐国|zh_min_nan|Kai-kok|zh_yue|甲斐國|zh|甲斐國|qid=Q858076|1=Kai Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.62169444|138.40561111}} == image (P18) == * HomijinnjyaIMG 1221.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q2280323|ltq=Q2280323|qid=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181666 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6090005002110 == Commons category (P373) == * Homi-jinja (Kushigata) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039702|ltq=Q135039702|qid=Q135039702|1=Homino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山梨県南アルプス市高尾498', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Homi-jinja (Kushigata)}} [[JA:穂見神社 (南アルプス市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11597095}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Homi Shrine |画像 = [[Image:HomijinnjyaIMG 1221.JPG|270px]]
Homi Shrine Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|qid=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minami-Alps|Q824000|qid=Q824000|1=Minami-Alps, Yamanashi}}498 Takao |位置 = {{coord|35|37|19.7|N|138|24|18.3|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}}| |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|qid=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Nagare-zukuri |例祭　 = {{ill|lt=November 22|Q3025|qid=Q3025|1=November 22}} |神事 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|lat_deg=35|lat_min=37|lat_sec=19.7|lon_deg=138|lon_min=24|lon_sec=18.3|label=Homi Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|37|19.7|N|138|24|18.3|E}}}} }} '''Homi Shrine''' (ほみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Takao (Minami-Alps)|UNKNOWN|lt=Takao}}, {{ill|lt=Minami-Alps|Q824000|qid=Q824000|1=Minami-Alps, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|qid=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. === Overview === '''Homi Shrine''' stands in Takao, a mountain settlement on the northeastern slope of {{ill|lt=Kushigatayama (Yamanashi)|Q11544680|qid=Q11544680|lt=Mount Kushigata|1=Mount Kushigata}}, in {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|qid=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minami-Alps|Q824000|qid=Q824000|1=Minami-Alps, Yamanashi}}. It is commonly called '''Takaozan Homi Shrine''', "Takao-san," or "Yama no Takao"[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/list/detail_no/5098 Yamanashi Shrine Office] and [http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/shigaku/documents/240401syukyohojinmeibo.pdf Religious corporations in Yamanashi (official Yamanashi Prefecture site)]. Its former rank is {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' lists a "Homi Shrine" among Kai Province {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}; besides this shrine, several other shrines named Homi Shrine exist in Yamanashi (including {{ill|lt=Nirasaki|Q290326|qid=Q290326|1=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}} and {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q1204276|qid=Q1204276|1=Shōwa, Yamanashi}}), and this shrine is considered one possible identification of that Engishiki shrine. It is also widely known in and outside the prefecture as a shrine for business prosperity, conferral of capital, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sericulture|1=UNKNOWN}}. Takao-kō confraternities exist as far away as Tokyo and Nagano, and worshippers visit from afar. In the past, the {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Kōtsū tram line|Q11469607|qid=Q11469607|1=Yamanashi Kōtsū tram line}} ("Boro-den") had {{ill|lt=Sōkomachi Station|Q11386643|qid=Q11386643|1=Sōkomachi Station}} in former Kushigata Town and was used by pilgrims. === Architecture === The '''{{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|qid=Q2044994|1=Honden}}''' is a sangen-sha nagare-zukuri building: 5.10 m beam length, 3.20 m depth, ridge height 9.30 m, floor area 25.91 m². It stands on a rubble-stone platform with copper-plate roofing. The current honden is an early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}} robust structure retaining {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Momoyama-period|1=UNKNOWN}} style, rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1665|Q7582|qid=Q7582|1=1665}}). In recent years, roof copper sheets were re-laid. It was designated a prefectural tangible cultural property in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|qid=Q2650|1=1965}}). The '''{{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|qid=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}}''' in the precinct was built in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 24 ({{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|qid=Q7837|1=1891}}), one-bay by one-bay, four-sided {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|qid=Q977293|1=Karahafu}} on the eaves. Carvings of the {{ill|lt=Four Symbols|Q1073260|qid=Q1073260|1=Four Symbols}} are placed on all four sides, giving it an ornate appearance. Also dedicated in the precinct is the "Horse of Takao," said to be the work of Edo-period carver Hidari Jingorō. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|qid=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}. === Annual festival === The '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daidai Kagura|1=UNKNOWN}}''' held every year on November 22 is designated an intangible folk cultural property of Minami-Alps City and draws many visitors. === Cultural properties === === Yamanashi Prefecture designated tangible cultural properties === * Homi Shrine honden (with ridge tags) * Homi Shrine bronze kakebotoke ("male deity mirror image," goshōtai) : A {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} {{ill|lt=mirror image|Q2703478|qid=Q2703478|1=Mirror image}}'Treasures of Yamanashi,' p.129, cast in bronze. Mirror diameter 26.4 cm, rim thickness 0.5 cm. Inscriptions on both sides of the mirror face include a date of {{ill|lt=Tenpuku|Q1194452|qid=Q1194452|lt=Tenpuku|1=Tenpuku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1233|Q5421|qid=Q5421|1=1233}}). A slightly curved circular mirror face bears an incised seated image of a male deity. Two cast lugs with circular perforations appear at the top. The inscription includes "Santai Ōji," interpreted as referring to "Daifuku Ōji, Daijumyō Ōji, Daichitoku Ōji" in vol.67 "Misaki Myōjin, Takao Village" of '{{ill|lt=Kai Kokushi|Q11577871|qid=Q11577871|1=Kaikokushi}}'. The back also bears the name "Daikanjin Renkōbō Benkei". === Minami-Alps City designated cultural properties === * Takao Homi Shrine Kaguraden (tangible cultural property) * Takao Homi Shrine Daidai Kagura (intangible folk cultural property) * Great cedar of Takao Homi Shrine (natural monument) === Gallery === 画像:保見DVC00150.JPG|First torii 画像:HomijinnjyaDVC00149.JPG|Second torii 画像:Homijinnjya kaguradenIMG 1187.JPG|Kaguraden 画像:穂見神社DVC00144.JPG|Precinct === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Homi Shrine (disambiguation)|UNKNOWN|lt=Homi Shrine}} - other shrines of the same name {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Homi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Homi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Homi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Minami-Alps]] [[Category:Buildings in Minami-Alps]]
